layman_headline: Market fear is rising as a global tech selloff drags crypto down — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is sliding because a massive selloff in AI and chip stocks is spreading into risk assets like Bitcoin and altcoins. Bitcoin dropped below $63,000 and most altcoins are down 5-20% this week. The Fear & Greed Index sits at 27, meaning investors are scared. While fear can create buying opportunities, the selling pressure hasn't shown clear signs of stopping yet.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines for now. The market is dropping due to forces outside crypto (tech stock selloff), and it's unclear when the bleeding will stop. Set alerts for Ethereum around $1,750 and Bitcoin around $61,500 — those could be strong bounce zones worth buying.
expert_reasoning: No candidates present a compelling risk/reward for immediate entry. ETH (RSI 35) is the closest to an actionable setup — approaching oversold with a bullish candle pattern and +2.17% 7-day hold despite today's -2.3% drop. However, price is below all major MAs (bearish structure), the chart bias score is 0.0 (neutral), and the R/R to nearest levels is only 0.36x. DEXE has a strong chart (+1.1 bias) but RSI at 85 screams overbought — a classic trap. LDO's +21% weekly move with a still-bearish MA structure suggests a dead-cat bounce. The macro environment — risk-off wave from AI stock selloff spreading into crypto — argues against catching falling knives. BTC dominance at 56.2% confirms capital is rotating away from alts. Best to wait for fear to peak and see a clear reversal candle on ETH near $1,700-$1,750 support.
risk_factors: Global tech/AI selloff could intensify further, dragging BTC below $61,000 and crushing alts
BTC dominance at 56.2% and rising means altcoins face additional relative underperformance
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear (<20) where capitulation bottoms typically form
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $61,500 support — a break below could trigger cascading liquidations
ETH RSI dropping below 30 (true oversold) would be the entry signal we need
Bitcoin ETF flow data over the next 48 hours — sustained inflows signal institutional bottom-fishing
market_context: A risk-off wave driven by the global AI/chip stock selloff is pulling Bitcoin below $63K and hammering altcoins. Fear & Greed at 27 is improving from 25 but hasn't reached capitulation levels, while BTC dominance at 56.2% shows money fleeing alts for relative safety.
context_banner: Global tech selloff is pressuring crypto — patience beats FOMO in a falling market.

key_signals.description: Risk-off wave from AI/chip stock selloff is explicitly dragging Bitcoin below $63K and hitting alts like HYPE (-10%), confirming macro headwinds are the dominant force.
ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed below 30 for a high-conviction entry.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) improving from 25 suggests sentiment is stabilizing but hasn't reached extreme fear levels where strong bounces typically originate.
Bitcoin ETFs added $368M in a 3-day buying streak, signaling institutional accumulation even during the selloff — a bullish undercurrent for when macro pressure eases.
Ether falling twice as hard as Bitcoin during the selloff confirms the high BTC dominance trend and warns against premature altcoin entries.
